campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's money management for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and the overall budget status. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including walking, biking, and public transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment and cash account performance
Monthly budget and treasurer's financial report
Multimodal transportation plan (walking, biking, transit improvements)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice opinions on a proposed new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council introduced the ordinance and skipped the usual first reading step to speed up the process, with plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Ordinance being placed on consent calendar for expedited approval

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes, reviewing the city's finances for January, and checking on progress with the General Plan and housing goals. The council also approved a final map for a new residential development called the Solana Project and discussed plans for community health and senior services.
keyTopics: Approved final map for Solana Project residential development
Reviewed January 2026 financial and investment reports
Checked progress on General Plan and Housing Element goals
Approved 2026 Community Health and Senior Services work plan
Updated meeting minute procedures and format

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council sessions. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for their April 7 meeting, decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked draft items for the April 14 meeting. The council also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda
Set time certain deadlines for specific agenda items
Reviewed new items being considered for the April 14 meeting
Checked notice requirements and waiver rules for agenda items
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 2, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's Human Relations Committee meeting scheduled for April 2, 2026 has been canceled. The committee will hold its next meeting on May 7, 2026.
keyTopics: Human Relations Committee meeting canceled
Next meeting rescheduled to May 7, 2026
No agenda items discussed at this session

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review funding applications for programs that help build affordable housing and support community services in Sunnyvale. The commission discussed how federal Community Development Block Grant money will be spent on housing and human services projects.
keyTopics: Federal housing and community development grant applications reviewed
Affordable housing funding priorities discussed
Human services program funding decisions
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 6, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 6, 2026 where residents were given time to speak about any city issues for up to 3 minutes each. The main business was electing new leaders for the Legislative Review Committee for 2026, and discussing the city's legislative calendar for the year.
keyTopics: Public comment period on city matters
Election of Legislative Review Committee Chair and Vice Chair
2026 legislative calendar planning
State law restrictions on decisions for items not on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Audit Committee met to review a report about how Silicon Valley Power handles its power purchase agreements. The meeting was held in a hybrid format, allowing residents to attend in person or join online via Zoom or phone.
keyTopics: City Auditor's report on Silicon Valley Power's power purchase agreement process
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote attendance options)
Public participation via Zoom webinar or phone dial-in

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss a new program that would help residents get security cameras for their homes through a grant. The council also reviewed the city's five-year spending plans for 2027-2031, including what infrastructure projects and services the city plans to fund.
keyTopics: Home Security Camera Grant Initiative - funding to help residents install security cameras
Five-Year Capital Improvement Program (2027-2031) - planned infrastructure and facility projects
Five-Year General Fund Financial Plan - how the city will budget and spend money
Meeting minutes and calendar approvals - routine council administrative matters

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council reviewed and approved the city's gas utility budget and financial plan for 2027. The council also voted to adjust residential and commercial gas rates based on the utility's financial needs.
keyTopics: Gas utility budget approval for fiscal year 2027
Changes to residential gas rates (Schedule G-1)
Changes to commercial gas rates (Schedules G-2 and G-3)
Transfer of money between utility reserves and general city fund
